/*
 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2025-2026 Espressif Systems (Shanghai) CO LTD
 *
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 */
/* Get Start Example

   This example code is in the Public Domain (or CC0 licensed, at your option.)

   Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, this
   software is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
   CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
*/
#include <stdio.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <unistd.h>

#include "nvs_flash.h"

#include "esp_mac.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "esp_wifi.h"
#include "esp_netif.h"
#include "esp_now.h"
#include "driver/gpio.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

#define CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL   11
#define SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO             GPIO_NUM_48

#if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C5 || CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C61 || (CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C6 && ESP_IDF_VERSION >= ESP_IDF_VERSION_VAL(5, 4, 0))
#define CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE   WIFI_BAND_MODE_2G_ONLY
#define CONFIG_WIFI_2G_BANDWIDTHS           WIFI_BW_HT40
#define CONFIG_WIFI_5G_BANDWIDTHS           WIFI_BW_HT40
#define CONFIG_WIFI_2G_PROTOCOL             WIFI_PROTOCOL_11N
#define CONFIG_WIFI_5G_PROTOCOL             WIFI_PROTOCOL_11N
#else
#define CONFIG_WIFI_BANDWIDTH           WIFI_BW_HT40
#endif

#define CONFIG_ESP_NOW_PHYMODE           WIFI_PHY_MODE_HT40
#define CONFIG_ESP_NOW_RATE             WIFI_PHY_RATE_MCS0_LGI
#define CONFIG_SEND_FREQUENCY               100

#if ESP_IDF_VERSION >= ESP_IDF_VERSION_VAL(6, 0, 0)
#define ESP_IF_WIFI_STA ESP_MAC_WIFI_STA
#endif

static const uint8_t CONFIG_CSI_SEND_MAC[] = {0x1a, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00};
static const char *TAG = "csi_send";

static void sender_status_led_task(void *arg)
{
    gpio_config_t io_conf = {
        .pin_bit_mask = 1ULL << SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO,
        .mode = GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT,
        .pull_up_en = GPIO_PULLUP_DISABLE,
        .pull_down_en = GPIO_PULLDOWN_DISABLE,
        .intr_type = GPIO_INTR_DISABLE,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(gpio_config(&io_conf));

    for (;;) {
        gpio_set_level(SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO, 1);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(250));
        gpio_set_level(SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO, 0);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(750));
    }
}

static void wifi_init()
{
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_event_loop_create_default());

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_netif_init());
    wifi_init_config_t cfg = WIFI_INIT_CONFIG_DEFAULT();
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_init(&cfg));

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_mode(WIFI_MODE_STA));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_storage(WIFI_STORAGE_RAM));

#if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C5
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_start());
    esp_wifi_set_band_mode(CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE);
    wifi_protocols_t protocols = {
        .ghz_2g = CONFIG_WIFI_2G_PROTOCOL,
        .ghz_5g = CONFIG_WIFI_5G_PROTOCOL
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_protocols(ESP_IF_WIFI_STA, &protocols));
    wifi_bandwidths_t bandwidth = {
        .ghz_2g = CONFIG_WIFI_2G_BANDWIDTHS,
        .ghz_5g = CONFIG_WIFI_5G_BANDWIDTHS
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_bandwidths(ESP_IF_WIFI_STA, &bandwidth));
#elif (CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C6 && ESP_IDF_VERSION >= ESP_IDF_VERSION_VAL(5, 4, 0)) || CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C61
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_start());
    esp_wifi_set_band_mode(CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE);
    wifi_protocols_t protocols = {
        .ghz_2g = CONFIG_WIFI_2G_PROTOCOL,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_protocols(ESP_IF_WIFI_STA, &protocols));
    wifi_bandwidths_t bandwidth = {
        .ghz_2g = CONFIG_WIFI_2G_BANDWIDTHS,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_bandwidths(ESP_IF_WIFI_STA, &bandwidth));
#else
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_bandwidth(ESP_IF_WIFI_STA, CONFIG_WIFI_BANDWIDTH));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_start());

#endif

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_ps(WIFI_PS_NONE));
#if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C5
    if ((CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE == WIFI_BAND_MODE_2G_ONLY && CONFIG_WIFI_2G_BANDWIDTHS == WIFI_BW_HT20)
            || (CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE == WIFI_BAND_MODE_5G_ONLY && CONFIG_WIFI_5G_BANDWIDTHS == WIFI_BW_HT20)) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_channel(CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL, WIFI_SECOND_CHAN_NONE));
    } else {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_channel(CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL, WIFI_SECOND_CHAN_BELOW));
    }
#elif (CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C6 && ESP_IDF_VERSION >= ESP_IDF_VERSION_VAL(5, 4, 0)) || CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C61
    if (CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE == WIFI_BAND_MODE_2G_ONLY && CONFIG_WIFI_2G_BANDWIDTHS == WIFI_BW_HT20) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_channel(CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL, WIFI_SECOND_CHAN_NONE));
    } else {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_channel(CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL, WIFI_SECOND_CHAN_BELOW));
    }
#else
    if (CONFIG_WIFI_BANDWIDTH == WIFI_BW_HT20) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_channel(CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL, WIFI_SECOND_CHAN_NONE));
    } else {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_channel(CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL, WIFI_SECOND_CHAN_BELOW));
    }
#endif
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_mac(WIFI_IF_STA, CONFIG_CSI_SEND_MAC));
}

static void wifi_esp_now_init(esp_now_peer_info_t peer)
{
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_now_init());
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_now_set_pmk((uint8_t *)"pmk1234567890123"));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_now_add_peer(&peer));
    esp_now_rate_config_t rate_config = {
        .phymode = CONFIG_ESP_NOW_PHYMODE,
        .rate = CONFIG_ESP_NOW_RATE,
        .ersu = false,
        .dcm = false
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_now_set_peer_rate_config(peer.peer_addr, &rate_config));
}

void app_main()
{
    /**
     * @brief Initialize NVS
     */
    esp_err_t ret = nvs_flash_init();
    if (ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NO_FREE_PAGES || ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NEW_VERSION_FOUND) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_flash_erase());
        ret = nvs_flash_init();
    }
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(ret);

    /**
     * @brief Initialize Wi-Fi
     */
    wifi_init();

    /**
     * @brief Initialize ESP-NOW
     *        ESP-NOW protocol see: https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/latest/esp32/api-reference/network/esp_now.html
     */
    esp_now_peer_info_t peer = {
        .channel   = CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL,
        .ifidx     = WIFI_IF_STA,
        .encrypt   = false,
        .peer_addr = {0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff},
    };
    wifi_esp_now_init(peer);

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "================ CSI SEND ================");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "wifi_channel: %d, send_frequency: %d, mac: " MACSTR,
             CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL, CONFIG_SEND_FREQUENCY, MAC2STR(CONFIG_CSI_SEND_MAC));
    xTaskCreate(sender_status_led_task, "sender_status_led", 2048, NULL, 1, NULL);

    for (uint32_t count = 0; ; ++count) {
        esp_err_t ret = esp_now_send(peer.peer_addr, (const uint8_t *)&count, sizeof(count));
        if (ret != ESP_OK) {
            ESP_LOGW(TAG, "free_heap: %ld <%s> ESP-NOW send error", esp_get_free_heap_size(), esp_err_to_name(ret));
        }

        usleep(1000 * 1000 / CONFIG_SEND_FREQUENCY);
    }
}

## Assistant

Flashed successfully.

New sender board:

```text
Port: /dev/cu.usbserial-0001
Chip: ESP32-S3
MAC: ac:a7:04:3d:2f:60
Firmware: csi_send
Transmit MAC used by firmware: 1a:00:00:00:00:00
Channel: 11
Send frequency: 100 packets/sec
```

Boot was confirmed in the monitor:

```text
csi_send: ================ CSI SEND ================
csi_send: wifi_channel: 11, send_frequency: 100, mac: 1a:00:00:00:00:00
```

There was one harmless warning: the board has 8MB flash, but the firmware image is configured for 2MB. That is fine for this sender app; it just means we are not using the extra flash space.

Use this new external-antenna board as the sender now, and keep the three Arduino Nano ESP32 boards as receivers.

## User

The board has a display attached. Add an animation of a security camera moving in the way that is typical to these cameras to the screen.

Status: Completed

## Assistant

I’ll add this directly to the sender firmware so the new board keeps transmitting ESP-NOW packets while the display runs its own small animation task. I’m going to assume this is a LilyGO-style ESP32-S3 display board with an ST7789 parallel LCD, which matches the detected ESP32-S3/display/external-antenna profile; if the screen stays dark, the pin map is the part we’ll adjust.

void example_init_lcd_panel(esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle, esp_lcd_panel_handle_t *panel)
{
    esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;
#if CONFIG_EXAMPLE_LCD_I80_CONTROLLER_ST7789
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Install LCD driver of st7789");
    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .reset_gpio_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_NUM_RST,
        .rgb_ele_order = LCD_RGB_ELEMENT_ORDER_RGB,
        .bits_per_pixel = 16,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_st7789(io_handle, &panel_config, &panel_handle));

    esp_lcd_panel_reset(panel_handle);
    esp_lcd_panel_init(panel_handle);
    // Set inversion, x/y coordinate order, x/y mirror according to your LCD module spec
    // the gap is LCD panel specific, even panels with the same driver IC, can have different gap value
    esp_lcd_panel_invert_color(panel_handle, true);
    esp_lcd_panel_set_gap(panel_handle, 0, 20);
#elif CONFIG_EXAMPLE_LCD_I80_CONTROLLER_NT35510
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Install LCD driver of nt35510");
    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .reset_gpio_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_NUM_RST,
        .rgb_ele_order = LCD_RGB_ELEMENT_ORDER_BGR,
        .bits_per_pixel = 16,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_nt35510(io_handle, &panel_config, &panel_handle));

    esp_lcd_panel_reset(panel_handle);
    esp_lcd_panel_init(panel_handle);
    // Set inversion, x/y coordinate order, x/y mirror according to your LCD module spec
    // the gap is LCD panel specific, even panels with the same driver IC, can have different gap value
    esp_lcd_panel_swap_xy(panel_handle, true);
    esp_lcd_panel_mirror(panel_handle, true, false);
#elif CONFIG_EXAMPLE_LCD_I80_CONTROLLER_ILI9341
    // ILI9341 is NOT a distinct driver, but a special case of ST7789


## Assistant

I’m changing the sender firmware now. The main hardware-sensitive part is the display pin map; I’m using the common T-Display-S3 ST7789 8-bit parallel map: data pins 39/40/41/42/45/46/47/48, control pins 5/6/7/8/9/15/38.

#include "esp_wifi.h"
#include "esp_netif.h"
#include "esp_now.h"
#include "esp_heap_caps.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_io.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_ops.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_vendor.h"
#include "driver/gpio.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

#define CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL   11
#define SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO             GPIO_NUM_48
#define CONFIG_SENDER_DISPLAY_ANIMATION    1

#define LCD_H_RES                          170
#define LCD_V_RES                          320
#define LCD_CHUNK_LINES                    16
#define LCD_PIXEL_CLOCK_HZ                 (10 * 1000 * 1000)
#define LCD_CMD_BITS                       8
#define LCD_PARAM_BITS                     8

#define LCD_PIN_NUM_PWR                    GPIO_NUM_15
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_BL                     GPIO_NUM_38
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_RST                    GPIO_NUM_5
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_CS                     GPIO_NUM_6
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DC                     GPIO_NUM_7
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_WR                     GPIO_NUM_8
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_RD                     GPIO_NUM_9
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA0                  GPIO_NUM_39
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA1                  GPIO_NUM_40
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA2                  GPIO_NUM_41
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA3                  GPIO_NUM_42
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA4                  GPIO_NUM_45
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA5                  GPIO_NUM_46
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA6                  GPIO_NUM_47
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA7                  GPIO_NUM_48
static const uint8_t CONFIG_CSI_SEND_MAC[] = {0x1a, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00};
static const char *TAG = "csi_send";

static inline uint16_t rgb565(uint8_t red, uint8_t green, uint8_t blue)
{
    return ((red & 0xf8) << 8) | ((green & 0xfc) << 3) | (blue >> 3);
}

static inline uint16_t blend_rgb565(uint16_t base, uint8_t red, uint8_t green, uint8_t blue, float alpha)
{
    alpha = alpha < 0.0f ? 0.0f : alpha;
    alpha = alpha > 1.0f ? 1.0f : alpha;

    uint8_t base_red = ((base >> 11) & 0x1f) << 3;
    uint8_t base_green = ((base >> 5) & 0x3f) << 2;
    uint8_t base_blue = (base & 0x1f) << 3;

    uint8_t out_red = base_red + (uint8_t)((red - base_red) * alpha);
    uint8_t out_green = base_green + (uint8_t)((green - base_green) * alpha);
    uint8_t out_blue = base_blue + (uint8_t)((blue - base_blue) * alpha);
    return rgb565(out_red, out_green, out_blue);
}

static inline float point_segment_distance(float px, float py, float ax, float ay, float bx, float by)
{
    float vx = bx - ax;
    float vy = by - ay;
    float wx = px - ax;
    float wy = py - ay;
    float len_sq = vx * vx + vy * vy;
    float t = len_sq > 0.0f ? (wx * vx + wy * vy) / len_sq : 0.0f;
    t = t < 0.0f ? 0.0f : t;
    t = t > 1.0f ? 1.0f : t;
    float dx = px - (ax + t * vx);
    float dy = py - (ay + t * vy);
    return sqrtf(dx * dx + dy * dy);
}

static uint16_t security_camera_pixel(int x, int y, float pan)
{
    uint16_t color = rgb565(3, 5, 7);
    float fx = (float)x;
    float fy = (float)y;
    float pivot_x = LCD_H_RES * 0.5f;
    float pivot_y = 86.0f;
    float dir_x = sinf(pan);
    float dir_y = cosf(pan);
    float perp_x = cosf(pan);
    float perp_y = -sinf(pan);
    float body_x = pivot_x + dir_x * 42.0f;
    float body_y = pivot_y + dir_y * 42.0f;
    float lens_x = body_x + dir_x * 35.0f;
    float lens_y = body_y + dir_y * 35.0f;

    float ray_x = fx - lens_x;
    float ray_y = fy - lens_y;
    float along = ray_x * dir_x + ray_y * dir_y;
    float side = fabsf(ray_x * perp_x + ray_y * perp_y);
    if (along > 0.0f && along < 210.0f) {
        float cone_width = 14.0f + along * 0.34f;
        if (side < cone_width) {
            float edge = 1.0f - side / cone_width;
            float fade = 1.0f - along / 210.0f;
            color = blend_rgb565(color, 34, 118, 118, 0.22f * edge * fade);
        }
    }

    if (point_segment_distance(fx, fy, pivot_x, 30.0f, pivot_x, pivot_y - 10.0f) < 3.0f ||
        point_segment_distance(fx, fy, pivot_x, pivot_y, body_x - dir_x * 35.0f, body_y - dir_y * 35.0f) < 4.0f) {
        color = rgb565(182, 190, 195);
    }

    float mount_dx = fx - pivot_x;
    float mount_dy = fy - pivot_y;
    float mount_dist = sqrtf(mount_dx * mount_dx + mount_dy * mount_dy);
    if (mount_dist < 15.0f) {
        color = mount_dist < 10.0f ? rgb565(210, 216, 220) : rgb565(88, 96, 104);
    }

    float bx = fx - body_x;
    float by = fy - body_y;
    float u = bx * dir_x + by * dir_y;
    float v = bx * perp_x + by * perp_y;
    if (u > -44.0f && u < 36.0f && fabsf(v) < 17.0f) {
        bool edge = u < -38.0f || u > 30.0f || fabsf(v) > 13.0f;
        color = edge ? rgb565(230, 235, 238) : rgb565(154, 164, 172);
    }

    float lens_dx = fx - lens_x;
    float lens_dy = fy - lens_y;
    float lens_dist = sqrtf(lens_dx * lens_dx + lens_dy * lens_dy);
    if (lens_dist < 15.0f) {
        color = lens_dist < 9.0f ? rgb565(9, 14, 20) : rgb565(232, 238, 240);
    }

    if (fabsf(fmodf(fy + 0.5f, 28.0f)) < 0.9f) {
        color = blend_rgb565(color, 255, 255, 255, 0.05f);
    }
    return color;
}

static esp_err_t sender_display_init(esp_lcd_panel_handle_t *panel)
{
    gpio_config_t output_conf = {
        .pin_bit_mask = (1ULL << LCD_PIN_NUM_PWR) | (1ULL << LCD_PIN_NUM_BL) | (1ULL << LCD_PIN_NUM_RD),
        .mode = GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT,
        .pull_up_en = GPIO_PULLUP_DISABLE,
        .pull_down_en = GPIO_PULLDOWN_DISABLE,
        .intr_type = GPIO_INTR_DISABLE,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(gpio_config(&output_conf));
    gpio_set_level(LCD_PIN_NUM_PWR, 1);
    gpio_set_level(LCD_PIN_NUM_RD, 1);
    gpio_set_level(LCD_PIN_NUM_BL, 1);

    esp_lcd_i80_bus_handle_t bus = NULL;
    esp_lcd_i80_bus_config_t bus_config = {
        .clk_src = LCD_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
        .dc_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_DC,
        .wr_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_WR,
        .data_gpio_nums = {
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA0,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA1,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA2,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA3,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA4,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA5,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA6,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA7,
        },
        .bus_width = 8,
        .max_transfer_bytes = LCD_H_RES * LCD_CHUNK_LINES * sizeof(uint16_t),
        .dma_burst_size = 64,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_new_i80_bus(&bus_config, &bus), TAG, "create LCD I80 bus");

    esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io = NULL;
    esp_lcd_panel_io_i80_config_t io_config = {
        .cs_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_CS,
        .pclk_hz = LCD_PIXEL_CLOCK_HZ,
        .trans_queue_depth = 10,
        .dc_levels = {
            .dc_idle_level = 0,
            .dc_cmd_level = 0,
            .dc_dummy_level = 0,
            .dc_data_level = 1,
        },
        .lcd_cmd_bits = LCD_CMD_BITS,
        .lcd_param_bits = LCD_PARAM_BITS,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_new_panel_io_i80(bus, &io_config, &io), TAG, "create LCD panel IO");

    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .reset_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_RST,
        .rgb_ele_order = LCD_RGB_ELEMENT_ORDER_RGB,
        .bits_per_pixel = 16,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_new_panel_st7789(io, &panel_config, panel), TAG, "create ST7789 panel");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_reset(*panel), TAG, "reset LCD panel");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_init(*panel), TAG, "init LCD panel");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_invert_color(*panel, true), TAG, "invert LCD color");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_set_gap(*panel, 0, 35), TAG, "set LCD gap");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(*panel, true), TAG, "turn LCD on");
    return ESP_OK;
}

static void sender_display_animation_task(void *arg)
{
    esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel = NULL;
    esp_err_t err = sender_display_init(&panel);
    if (err != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "display animation disabled: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
        vTaskDelete(NULL);
    }

    uint16_t *line_buffer = heap_caps_malloc(
        LCD_H_RES * LCD_CHUNK_LINES * sizeof(uint16_t),
        MALLOC_CAP_DMA | MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
    if (!line_buffer) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "display animation disabled: no DMA buffer");
        vTaskDelete(NULL);
    }

    float phase = 0.0f;
    for (;;) {
        float pan = sinf(phase) * 0.62f;
        for (int y0 = 0; y0 < LCD_V_RES; y0 += LCD_CHUNK_LINES) {
            int lines = LCD_CHUNK_LINES;
            if (y0 + lines > LCD_V_RES) {
                lines = LCD_V_RES - y0;
            }
            for (int y = 0; y < lines; ++y) {
                for (int x = 0; x < LCD_H_RES; ++x) {
                    line_buffer[y * LCD_H_RES + x] = security_camera_pixel(x, y0 + y, pan);
                }
            }
            ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(panel, 0, y0, LCD_H_RES, y0 + lines, line_buffer));
        }
        phase += 0.075f;
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(35));
    }
}

#if !CONFIG_SENDER_DISPLAY_ANIMATION
static void sender_status_led_task(void *arg)
{
    gpio_config_t io_conf = {
        .pin_bit_mask = 1ULL << SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO,
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(750));
    }
}
#endif

## Assistant

I added and flashed a display animation to the new sender board.

What changed:

- The board still runs `csi_send` at 100 packets/sec on channel 11.
- The old GPIO48 blink is disabled for this sender, because GPIO48 is likely part of the display data bus.
- A new display task draws a panning security-camera-style animation on the board screen.

Boot was confirmed:

```text
csi_send: ================ CSI SEND ================
csi_send: wifi_channel: 11, send_frequency: 100, mac: 1a:00:00:00:00:00
```

No display initialization errors appeared in the monitor.

If the screen is dark or the image is offset/garbled, the firmware is running but the board’s display pin map differs from the common T-Display-S3 map I used. In that case send me the exact board model or a close-up photo of the back/front labels, and I’ll adjust the pins.

## User

Sadly the display is dark- here are close ups of the board. They are ESP32 LoRA Boards previously used for meshtastic as far as i can tell.

## Assistant

That explains it. This is not the T-Display-S3 parallel LCD I guessed from the first description; it’s a Heltec WiFi LoRa 32-style board with a small 128x64 OLED. The display uses SSD1315/SSD1306-style I2C on GPIO17/18 with reset on GPIO21, so I’ll replace the ST7789 display code with a lightweight monochrome OLED driver.

#include <stdio.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <math.h>

#include "nvs_flash.h"

#include "esp_mac.h"
#include "esp_check.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "esp_wifi.h"
#include "esp_netif.h"
#include "esp_now.h"
#include "esp_heap_caps.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_io.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_ops.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_vendor.h"
#include "driver/gpio.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

#define CONFIG_LESS_INTERFERENCE_CHANNEL   11
#define SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO             GPIO_NUM_48
#define CONFIG_SENDER_DISPLAY_ANIMATION    1

#define LCD_H_RES                          170
#define LCD_V_RES                          320
#define LCD_CHUNK_LINES                    16
#define LCD_PIXEL_CLOCK_HZ                 (10 * 1000 * 1000)
#define LCD_CMD_BITS                       8
#define LCD_PARAM_BITS                     8

#define LCD_PIN_NUM_PWR                    GPIO_NUM_15
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_BL                     GPIO_NUM_38
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_RST                    GPIO_NUM_5
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_CS                     GPIO_NUM_6
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DC                     GPIO_NUM_7
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_WR                     GPIO_NUM_8
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_RD                     GPIO_NUM_9
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA0                  GPIO_NUM_39
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA1                  GPIO_NUM_40
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA2                  GPIO_NUM_41
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA3                  GPIO_NUM_42
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA4                  GPIO_NUM_45
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA5                  GPIO_NUM_46
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA6                  GPIO_NUM_47
#define LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA7                  GPIO_NUM_48

#if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C5 || CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C61 || (CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C6 && ESP_IDF_VERSION >= ESP_IDF_VERSION_VAL(5, 4, 0))
#define CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE   WIFI_BAND_MODE_2G_ONLY
#define CONFIG_WIFI_2G_BANDWIDTHS           WIFI_BW_HT40
#define CONFIG_WIFI_5G_BANDWIDTHS           WIFI_BW_HT40
#define CONFIG_WIFI_2G_PROTOCOL             WIFI_PROTOCOL_11N
#define CONFIG_WIFI_5G_PROTOCOL             WIFI_PROTOCOL_11N
#else
#define CONFIG_WIFI_BANDWIDTH           WIFI_BW_HT40
#endif

#define CONFIG_ESP_NOW_PHYMODE           WIFI_PHY_MODE_HT40
#define CONFIG_ESP_NOW_RATE             WIFI_PHY_RATE_MCS0_LGI
#define CONFIG_SEND_FREQUENCY               100

#if ESP_IDF_VERSION >= ESP_IDF_VERSION_VAL(6, 0, 0)
#define ESP_IF_WIFI_STA ESP_MAC_WIFI_STA
#endif

static const uint8_t CONFIG_CSI_SEND_MAC[] = {0x1a, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00};
static const char *TAG = "csi_send";

static inline uint16_t rgb565(uint8_t red, uint8_t green, uint8_t blue)
{
    return ((red & 0xf8) << 8) | ((green & 0xfc) << 3) | (blue >> 3);
}

static inline uint16_t blend_rgb565(uint16_t base, uint8_t red, uint8_t green, uint8_t blue, float alpha)
{
    alpha = alpha < 0.0f ? 0.0f : alpha;
    alpha = alpha > 1.0f ? 1.0f : alpha;

    uint8_t base_red = ((base >> 11) & 0x1f) << 3;
    uint8_t base_green = ((base >> 5) & 0x3f) << 2;
    uint8_t base_blue = (base & 0x1f) << 3;

    uint8_t out_red = base_red + (uint8_t)((red - base_red) * alpha);
    uint8_t out_green = base_green + (uint8_t)((green - base_green) * alpha);
    uint8_t out_blue = base_blue + (uint8_t)((blue - base_blue) * alpha);
    return rgb565(out_red, out_green, out_blue);
}

static inline float point_segment_distance(float px, float py, float ax, float ay, float bx, float by)
{
    float vx = bx - ax;
    float vy = by - ay;
    float wx = px - ax;
    float wy = py - ay;
    float len_sq = vx * vx + vy * vy;
    float t = len_sq > 0.0f ? (wx * vx + wy * vy) / len_sq : 0.0f;
    t = t < 0.0f ? 0.0f : t;
    t = t > 1.0f ? 1.0f : t;
    float dx = px - (ax + t * vx);
    float dy = py - (ay + t * vy);
    return sqrtf(dx * dx + dy * dy);
}

static uint16_t security_camera_pixel(int x, int y, float pan)
{
    uint16_t color = rgb565(3, 5, 7);
    float fx = (float)x;
    float fy = (float)y;
    float pivot_x = LCD_H_RES * 0.5f;
    float pivot_y = 86.0f;
    float dir_x = sinf(pan);
    float dir_y = cosf(pan);
    float perp_x = cosf(pan);
    float perp_y = -sinf(pan);
    float body_x = pivot_x + dir_x * 42.0f;
    float body_y = pivot_y + dir_y * 42.0f;
    float lens_x = body_x + dir_x * 35.0f;
    float lens_y = body_y + dir_y * 35.0f;

    float ray_x = fx - lens_x;
    float ray_y = fy - lens_y;
    float along = ray_x * dir_x + ray_y * dir_y;
    float side = fabsf(ray_x * perp_x + ray_y * perp_y);
    if (along > 0.0f && along < 210.0f) {
        float cone_width = 14.0f + along * 0.34f;
        if (side < cone_width) {
            float edge = 1.0f - side / cone_width;
            float fade = 1.0f - along / 210.0f;
            color = blend_rgb565(color, 34, 118, 118, 0.22f * edge * fade);
        }
    }

    if (point_segment_distance(fx, fy, pivot_x, 30.0f, pivot_x, pivot_y - 10.0f) < 3.0f ||
        point_segment_distance(fx, fy, pivot_x, pivot_y, body_x - dir_x * 35.0f, body_y - dir_y * 35.0f) < 4.0f) {
        color = rgb565(182, 190, 195);
    }

    float mount_dx = fx - pivot_x;
    float mount_dy = fy - pivot_y;
    float mount_dist = sqrtf(mount_dx * mount_dx + mount_dy * mount_dy);
    if (mount_dist < 15.0f) {
        color = mount_dist < 10.0f ? rgb565(210, 216, 220) : rgb565(88, 96, 104);
    }

    float bx = fx - body_x;
    float by = fy - body_y;
    float u = bx * dir_x + by * dir_y;
    float v = bx * perp_x + by * perp_y;
    if (u > -44.0f && u < 36.0f && fabsf(v) < 17.0f) {
        bool edge = u < -38.0f || u > 30.0f || fabsf(v) > 13.0f;
        color = edge ? rgb565(230, 235, 238) : rgb565(154, 164, 172);
    }

    float lens_dx = fx - lens_x;
    float lens_dy = fy - lens_y;
    float lens_dist = sqrtf(lens_dx * lens_dx + lens_dy * lens_dy);
    if (lens_dist < 15.0f) {
        color = lens_dist < 9.0f ? rgb565(9, 14, 20) : rgb565(232, 238, 240);
    }

    if (fabsf(fmodf(fy + 0.5f, 28.0f)) < 0.9f) {
        color = blend_rgb565(color, 255, 255, 255, 0.05f);
    }
    return color;
}

static esp_err_t sender_display_init(esp_lcd_panel_handle_t *panel)
{
    gpio_config_t output_conf = {
        .pin_bit_mask = (1ULL << LCD_PIN_NUM_PWR) | (1ULL << LCD_PIN_NUM_BL) | (1ULL << LCD_PIN_NUM_RD),
        .mode = GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT,
        .pull_up_en = GPIO_PULLUP_DISABLE,
        .pull_down_en = GPIO_PULLDOWN_DISABLE,
        .intr_type = GPIO_INTR_DISABLE,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(gpio_config(&output_conf));
    gpio_set_level(LCD_PIN_NUM_PWR, 1);
    gpio_set_level(LCD_PIN_NUM_RD, 1);
    gpio_set_level(LCD_PIN_NUM_BL, 1);

    esp_lcd_i80_bus_handle_t bus = NULL;
    esp_lcd_i80_bus_config_t bus_config = {
        .clk_src = LCD_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
        .dc_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_DC,
        .wr_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_WR,
        .data_gpio_nums = {
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA0,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA1,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA2,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA3,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA4,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA5,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA6,
            LCD_PIN_NUM_DATA7,
        },
        .bus_width = 8,
        .max_transfer_bytes = LCD_H_RES * LCD_CHUNK_LINES * sizeof(uint16_t),
        .dma_burst_size = 64,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_new_i80_bus(&bus_config, &bus), TAG, "create LCD I80 bus");

    esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io = NULL;
    esp_lcd_panel_io_i80_config_t io_config = {
        .cs_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_CS,
        .pclk_hz = LCD_PIXEL_CLOCK_HZ,
        .trans_queue_depth = 10,
        .dc_levels = {
            .dc_idle_level = 0,
            .dc_cmd_level = 0,
            .dc_dummy_level = 0,
            .dc_data_level = 1,
        },
        .lcd_cmd_bits = LCD_CMD_BITS,
        .lcd_param_bits = LCD_PARAM_BITS,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_new_panel_io_i80(bus, &io_config, &io), TAG, "create LCD panel IO");

    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .reset_gpio_num = LCD_PIN_NUM_RST,
        .rgb_ele_order = LCD_RGB_ELEMENT_ORDER_RGB,
        .bits_per_pixel = 16,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_new_panel_st7789(io, &panel_config, panel), TAG, "create ST7789 panel");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_reset(*panel), TAG, "reset LCD panel");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_init(*panel), TAG, "init LCD panel");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_invert_color(*panel, true), TAG, "invert LCD color");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_set_gap(*panel, 0, 35), TAG, "set LCD gap");
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(*panel, true), TAG, "turn LCD on");
    return ESP_OK;
}

static void sender_display_animation_task(void *arg)
{
    esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel = NULL;
    esp_err_t err = sender_display_init(&panel);
    if (err != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "display animation disabled: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
        vTaskDelete(NULL);
    }

    uint16_t *line_buffer = heap_caps_malloc(
        LCD_H_RES * LCD_CHUNK_LINES * sizeof(uint16_t),
        MALLOC_CAP_DMA | MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
    if (!line_buffer) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "display animation disabled: no DMA buffer");
        vTaskDelete(NULL);
    }

    float phase = 0.0f;
    for (;;) {
        float pan = sinf(phase) * 0.62f;
        for (int y0 = 0; y0 < LCD_V_RES; y0 += LCD_CHUNK_LINES) {
            int lines = LCD_CHUNK_LINES;
            if (y0 + lines > LCD_V_RES) {
                lines = LCD_V_RES - y0;
            }
            for (int y = 0; y < lines; ++y) {
                for (int x = 0; x < LCD_H_RES; ++x) {
                    line_buffer[y * LCD_H_RES + x] = security_camera_pixel(x, y0 + y, pan);
                }
            }
            ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(panel, 0, y0, LCD_H_RES, y0 + lines, line_buffer));
        }
        phase += 0.075f;
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(35));
    }
}

#if !CONFIG_SENDER_DISPLAY_ANIMATION
static void sender_status_led_task(void *arg)
{
    gpio_config_t io_conf = {
        .pin_bit_mask = 1ULL << SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO,
        .mode = GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT,
        .pull_up_en = GPIO_PULLUP_DISABLE,
        .pull_down_en = GPIO_PULLDOWN_DISABLE,
        .intr_type = GPIO_INTR_DISABLE,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(gpio_config(&io_conf));

    for (;;) {
        gpio_set_level(SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO, 1);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(250));
        gpio_set_level(SENDER_STATUS_LED_GPIO, 0);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(750));
    }
}
#endif

static void wifi_init()
{
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_event_loop_create_default());

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_netif_init());
    wifi_init_config_t cfg = WIFI_INIT_CONFIG_DEFAULT();
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_init(&cfg));

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_mode(WIFI_MODE_STA));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_storage(WIFI_STORAGE_RAM));

#if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C5
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_start());
    esp_wifi_set_band_mode(CONFIG_WIFI_BAND_MODE);
    wifi_protocols_t protocols = {
        .ghz_2g = CONFIG_WIFI_2G_PROTOCOL,
        .ghz_5g = CONFIG_WIFI_5G_PROTOCOL
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_protocols(ESP_IF_WIFI_STA, &protocols));
    wifi_bandwidths_t bandwidth = {
        .ghz_2g = CONFIG_WIFI_2G_BANDWIDTHS,
        .ghz_5g = CONFIG_WIFI_5G_BANDWIDTHS
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_set_bandwidths(ESP_IF_WIFI_STA, &bandwidth));
#elif (CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C6 && ESP_IDF_VERSION >= ESP_IDF_VERSION_VAL(5, 4, 0)) || CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32C61
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_wifi_start());

void app_main(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initialize I2C bus");
    i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus = NULL;
    i2c_master_bus_config_t bus_config = {
        .clk_source = I2C_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
        .glitch_ignore_cnt = 7,
        .i2c_port = I2C_BUS_PORT,
        .sda_io_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_NUM_SDA,
        .scl_io_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_NUM_SCL,
        .flags.enable_internal_pullup = true,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2c_new_master_bus(&bus_config, &i2c_bus));

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Install panel IO");
    esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
    esp_lcd_panel_io_i2c_config_t io_config = {
        .dev_addr = EXAMPLE_I2C_HW_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = EXAMPLE_LCD_PIXEL_CLOCK_HZ,
        .control_phase_bytes = 1,               // According to SSD1306 datasheet
        .lcd_cmd_bits = EXAMPLE_LCD_CMD_BITS,   // According to SSD1306 datasheet
        .lcd_param_bits = EXAMPLE_LCD_CMD_BITS, // According to SSD1306 datasheet
#if CONFIG_EXAMPLE_LCD_CONTROLLER_SSD1306
        .dc_bit_offset = 6,                     // According to SSD1306 datasheet
#elif CONFIG_EXAMPLE_LCD_CONTROLLER_SH1107
        .dc_bit_offset = 0,                     // According to SH1107 datasheet
        .flags =
        {
            .disable_control_phase = 1,
        }
#endif
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_io_i2c(i2c_bus, &io_config, &io_handle));

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Install SSD1306 panel driver");
    esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;
    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .bits_per_pixel = 1,
        .reset_gpio_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_NUM_RST,
    };
#if CONFIG_EXAMPLE_LCD_CONTROLLER_SSD1306
    esp_lcd_panel_ssd1306_config_t ssd1306_config = {
        .height = EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES,
    };
    panel_config.vendor_config = &ssd1306_config;
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_ssd1306(io_handle, &panel_config, &panel_handle));

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_sh1107(io_handle, &panel_config, &panel_handle));
#endif

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_reset(panel_handle));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_init(panel_handle));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(panel_handle, true));

#if CONFIG_EXAMPLE_LCD_CONTROLLER_SH1107
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_invert_color(panel_handle, true));
#endif

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initialize LVGL");
    lv_init();
    // create a lvgl display
    lv_display_t *display = lv_display_create(EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES, EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES);
    // associate the i2c panel handle to the display

#define CONFIG_SENDER_DISPLAY_ANIMATION    1

#define OLED_PAGES                         (OLED_HEIGHT / 8)
#define OLED_BUFFER_SIZE                   (OLED_WIDTH * OLED_PAGES)
#define OLED_I2C_ADDR                      0x3c
#define OLED_I2C_FREQ_HZ                   400000

#define OLED_PIN_NUM_SCL                   GPIO_NUM_18
#define OLED_PIN_NUM_RST                   GPIO_NUM_21

```
static const uint8_t CONFIG_CSI_SEND_MAC[] = {0x1a, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00};
static const char *TAG = "csi_send";

static inline float point_segment_distance(float px, float py, float ax, float ay, float bx, float by)
{
    float vx = bx - ax;
    float vy = by - ay;
    return sqrtf(dx * dx + dy * dy);
}

static void oled_set_pixel(uint8_t *frame, int x, int y, bool on)
{
    if (x < 0 || x >= OLED_WIDTH || y < 0 || y >= OLED_HEIGHT) {
        return;
    }
    uint16_t index = (y / 8) * OLED_WIDTH + x;
    uint8_t mask = 1U << (y % 8);
    if (on) {
        frame[index] |= mask;
    } else {
        frame[index] &= ~mask;
    }
}

static void oled_draw_disc(uint8_t *frame, float cx, float cy, float radius)
{
    int min_x = (int)(cx - radius - 1.0f);
    int max_x = (int)(cx + radius + 1.0f);
    int min_y = (int)(cy - radius - 1.0f);
    int max_y = (int)(cy + radius + 1.0f);
    float r2 = radius * radius;
    for (int y = min_y; y <= max_y; ++y) {
        for (int x = min_x; x <= max_x; ++x) {
            float dx = (float)x - cx;
            float dy = (float)y - cy;
            if (dx * dx + dy * dy <= r2) {
                oled_set_pixel(frame, x, y, true);
            }
        }
    }
}

static void oled_draw_line(uint8_t *frame, float ax, float ay, float bx, float by, float width)
{
    int min_x = (int)fminf(ax, bx) - (int)width - 2;
    int max_x = (int)fmaxf(ax, bx) + (int)width + 2;
    int min_y = (int)fminf(ay, by) - (int)width - 2;
    int max_y = (int)fmaxf(ay, by) + (int)width + 2;
    for (int y = min_y; y <= max_y; ++y) {
        for (int x = min_x; x <= max_x; ++x) {
            if (point_segment_distance((float)x, (float)y, ax, ay, bx, by) <= width) {
                oled_set_pixel(frame, x, y, true);
            }
        }
    }
}

static void oled_draw_rotated_rect(uint8_t *frame, float cx, float cy, float dir_x, float dir_y,
                                   float half_len, float half_width, bool filled)
{
    float perp_x = dir_y;
    float perp_y = -dir_x;
    int min_x = (int)(cx - half_len - half_width - 2.0f);
    int max_x = (int)(cx + half_len + half_width + 2.0f);
    int min_y = (int)(cy - half_len - half_width - 2.0f);
    int max_y = (int)(cy + half_len + half_width + 2.0f);
    for (int y = min_y; y <= max_y; ++y) {
        for (int x = min_x; x <= max_x; ++x) {
            float dx = (float)x - cx;
            float dy = (float)y - cy;
            float u = dx * dir_x + dy * dir_y;
            float v = dx * perp_x + dy * perp_y;
            bool inside = fabsf(u) <= half_len && fabsf(v) <= half_width;
            bool edge = fabsf(fabsf(u) - half_len) < 1.3f || fabsf(fabsf(v) - half_width) < 1.3f;
            if (inside && (filled || edge)) {
                oled_set_pixel(frame, x, y, true);
            }
        }
    }
}

static void draw_security_camera_frame(uint8_t *frame, float pan)
{
    memset(frame, 0, OLED_BUFFER_SIZE);

    float pivot_x = OLED_WIDTH * 0.5f;
    float pivot_y = 17.0f;
    float dir_x = sinf(pan);
    float dir_y = cosf(pan);
    float perp_x = cosf(pan);
    float perp_y = -sinf(pan);

    float body_x = pivot_x + dir_x * 17.0f;
    float body_y = pivot_y + dir_y * 17.0f;
    float lens_x = body_x + dir_x * 18.0f;
    float lens_y = body_y + dir_y * 18.0f;

    for (int y = 0; y < OLED_HEIGHT; ++y) {
        for (int x = 0; x < OLED_WIDTH; ++x) {
            float ray_x = (float)x - lens_x;
            float ray_y = (float)y - lens_y;
            float along = ray_x * dir_x + ray_y * dir_y;
            float side = fabsf(ray_x * perp_x + ray_y * perp_y);
            float cone_width = 3.0f + along * 0.42f;
            bool cone_edge = along > 0.0f && along < 88.0f &&
                             side > cone_width - 1.4f && side < cone_width + 0.8f;
            bool cone_fill = along > 0.0f && along < 74.0f &&
                             side < cone_width && ((x + y + (int)(pan * 20.0f)) % 9 == 0);
            if (cone_edge || cone_fill) {
                oled_set_pixel(frame, x, y, true);
            }
        }
    }

    oled_draw_line(frame, pivot_x, 1.0f, pivot_x, pivot_y - 7.0f, 1.1f);
    oled_draw_line(frame, pivot_x, pivot_y, body_x - dir_x * 15.0f, body_y - dir_y * 15.0f, 1.3f);
    oled_draw_disc(frame, pivot_x, pivot_y, 5.6f);
    oled_draw_disc(frame, pivot_x, pivot_y, 2.2f);
    oled_draw_rotated_rect(frame, body_x, body_y, dir_x, dir_y, 21.0f, 7.0f, false);
    oled_draw_rotated_rect(frame, body_x + dir_x * 4.0f, body_y + dir_y * 4.0f, dir_x, dir_y, 16.0f, 4.6f, true);
    oled_draw_disc(frame, lens_x, lens_y, 7.0f);
    oled_draw_disc(frame, lens_x, lens_y, 3.2f);

    for (int x = 10; x < OLED_WIDTH - 10; x += 8) {
        oled_set_pixel(frame, x, 60, true);
        oled_set_pixel(frame, x + 1, 60, true);
    }
}

static esp_err_t oled_write(i2c_master_dev_handle_t dev, uint8_t control, const uint8_t *data, size_t len)
{
    uint8_t tx[OLED_BUFFER_SIZE + 1];
    if (len > OLED_BUFFER_SIZE) {
        return ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
    }
    tx[0] = control;
    memcpy(&tx[1], data, len);
    return i2c_master_transmit(dev, tx, len + 1, 1000);
}

static esp_err_t oled_command(i2c_master_dev_handle_t dev, const uint8_t *commands, size_t len)
{
    return oled_write(dev, 0x00, commands, len);
}

static esp_err_t oled_data(i2c_master_dev_handle_t dev, const uint8_t *data, size_t len)
{
    return oled_write(dev, 0x40, data, len);
}

static esp_err_t sender_display_init(i2c_master_dev_handle_t *dev)
{
    gpio_config_t rst_conf = {
        .pin_bit_mask = 1ULL << OLED_PIN_NUM_RST,
        .mode = GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT,
        .pull_up_en = GPIO_PULLUP_DISABLE,
        .pull_down_en = GPIO_PULLDOWN_DISABLE,
        .intr_type = GPIO_INTR_DISABLE,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(gpio_config(&rst_conf));
    gpio_set_level(OLED_PIN_NUM_RST, 0);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(20));
    gpio_set_level(OLED_PIN_NUM_RST, 1);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(20));

    i2c_master_bus_handle_t bus = NULL;
    i2c_master_bus_config_t bus_config = {
        .clk_source = I2C_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
        .i2c_port = I2C_NUM_0,
        .sda_io_num = OLED_PIN_NUM_SDA,
        .scl_io_num = OLED_PIN_NUM_SCL,
        .glitch_ignore_cnt = 7,
        .flags.enable_internal_pullup = true,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(i2c_new_master_bus(&bus_config, &bus), TAG, "create OLED I2C bus");

    i2c_device_config_t dev_config = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = OLED_I2C_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = OLED_I2C_FREQ_HZ,
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(i2c_master_bus_add_device(bus, &dev_config, dev), TAG, "add OLED I2C device");

    const uint8_t init[] = {
        0xAE,       // display off
        0xD5, 0x80, // clock
        0xA8, 0x3F, // multiplex 64
        0xD3, 0x00, // offset
        0x40,       // start line
        0x8D, 0x14, // charge pump
        0x20, 0x00, // horizontal addressing
        0xA1,       // segment remap
        0xC8,       // COM scan direction
        0xDA, 0x12, // COM pins
        0x81, 0xCF, // contrast
        0xD9, 0xF1, // precharge
        0xDB, 0x40, // vcomh
        0xA4,       // resume RAM content display
        0xA6,       // normal display
        0x2E,       // deactivate scroll
        0xAF,       // display on
    };
    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(oled_command(*dev, init, sizeof(init)), TAG, "initialize OLED");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OLED animation initialized on SDA=%d SCL=%d RST=%d", OLED_PIN_NUM_SDA, OLED_PIN_NUM_SCL, OLED_PIN_NUM_RST);
    return ESP_OK;
}

static void sender_display_animation_task(void *arg)
{
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t oled = NULL;
    esp_err_t err = sender_display_init(&oled);
    if (err != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "display animation disabled: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
        vTaskDelete(NULL);
    }

    uint8_t frame[OLED_BUFFER_SIZE];
    const uint8_t window[] = {
        0x21, 0x00, OLED_WIDTH - 1,
        0x22, 0x00, OLED_PAGES - 1,
    };

    float phase = 0.0f;
    for (;;) {
        float pan = sinf(phase) * 0.72f;
        draw_security_camera_frame(frame, pan);
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(oled_command(oled, window, sizeof(window)));
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(oled_data(oled, frame, sizeof(frame)));
        phase += 0.105f;
        if (phase > 6.283185f) {
            phase -= 6.283185f;
        }
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(38));
    }
}
```


## Assistant

The firmware now targets the Heltec OLED: SSD1315/SSD1306-compatible 128x64 over I2C. I’m building it before flashing so we catch any ESP-IDF API mismatch locally.

## Assistant

Good catch. The board is a Heltec WiFi LoRa 32-style board, not the color LCD board I initially assumed.

I replaced the display code with the correct OLED driver path:

```text
OLED: SSD1315 / SSD1306 style
Resolution: 128x64 monochrome
I2C SDA: GPIO17
I2C SCL: GPIO18
OLED reset: GPIO21
```

Flashed successfully, and the monitor confirms:

```text
csi_send: OLED animation initialized on SDA=17 SCL=18 RST=21
```

The board is still running as the CSI sender:

```text
wifi_channel: 11
send_frequency: 100
mac: 1a:00:00:00:00:00
```

You should now see a small monochrome security-camera pan animation on the OLED.

## User

Lets move on. I have installed the new sender in the position of the old one. I have connected all the receivers again. Test if everything is working.

## Assistant

I’ll verify the whole chain in layers: first that all serial ports are present, then that the receiver firmware is producing CSI rows from the new sender, and finally a short three-receiver baseline capture to confirm the host tools can read all receivers together.

## Assistant

The new sender works electrically, but the localization result got worse with the current geometry.

Results:

```text
left_zone   -> center  confidence 0.65
center_zone -> center  confidence 0.87
right_zone  -> center  confidence 0.64
```

So the center receiver is dominating everything now. That matches the earlier signal check: center is much stronger than left/right with the external-antenna sender.

Current RSSI pattern during this test:

```text
center around -60 dBm
left/right around -69 to -73 dBm
```

That means the new sender is likely aimed or positioned so its strongest path favors the center receiver too much.

Before changing code, adjust geometry:

1. Move the sender slightly farther back from the receiver row.
2. Aim the external antenna less directly at the center receiver.
3. If the antenna is vertical now, try rotating/angling it slightly so left/right receive more comparable signal.
4. Keep the three receivers unchanged.

Goal for baseline RSSI is not equal, but closer. Something like:

```text
left   -65 to -72
center -60 to -68
right  -65 to -72
```

Right now center has too much advantage. After adjusting sender angle/position, rerun just a short `chain_check` first before another full left/center/right test.

## User

does this center bias affect csi data as well or just the current rssi setup?

## Assistant

It affects both.

RSSI is just the easy diagnostic that shows the bias clearly:

```text
center much stronger than left/right
```

But the CSI data comes from the same received packets and same RF path. If the center receiver has a much stronger, cleaner, more direct path from the sender, then its CSI amplitude changes will often dominate too.

That is exactly what happened in the test:

```text
left_zone   -> center
center_zone -> center
right_zone  -> center
```

So this is not just an RSSI display issue. The CSI heuristic is also being pulled toward the center because the center receiver sees the strongest and most sensitive channel.

Important nuance: stronger signal is not always better for localization. For this prototype, we want the three receiver paths to be **comparably sensitive** to a person in their zone. The center can be somewhat stronger, but not so strong that every body position changes center CSI most.

So use RSSI as a quick setup proxy:

- If one receiver is 10-15 dB stronger than the others, expect CSI localization bias.
- If RSSI is closer, CSI zone scoring usually behaves better.
- Final judgment still comes from the CSI zone test, not RSSI alone.

## User

  This is the current receiver array and an overview of the other side of the wall with the sender board attached horizontally on the leftmost edge of the railing on the overview picture.This is the current receiver array and an overview of the other side of the wall with the sender board attached horizontally on the leftmost edge of the railing on the overview picture. Suggest changes to the setup to increase accuracy.  

## Assistant

The main issue is geometry: your sender is far left and horizontally mounted on a metal railing, while the receiver array is clustered around the center/right side. That will create a strong asymmetric path and lots of railing reflections.

Best changes, in priority order:

1. Move the sender closer to the horizontal center of the receiver array  
Right now the sender is on the left edge of the railing. Put it roughly opposite the middle receiver if possible. This is the biggest likely improvement.

2. Get the sender away from metal  
Do not attach the antenna/board directly to the metal railing. Use tape/foam/cardboard/wood to offset it at least 10-20 cm from metal. Metal near the antenna will distort the radiation pattern and add strong reflections.

3. Make the sender vertical, not horizontal  
Mount the Heltec board upright, with the external antenna vertical. Keep the antenna clear of the railing and wall. This gives a more predictable polarization relative to the receivers.

4. Raise the receivers and keep them at equal height  
The receivers currently look low and partly embedded in plastic bins/chair structures. Put all three at the same height, ideally around torso height for the person being sensed.

5. Remove the red plastic bins if possible  
They are useful holders, but they create cavities and edges around the boards. Use simpler stands or tape the boards to cardboard/foam so the antenna area has open space.

6. Separate the receivers more evenly  
They should form a clean row:

```text
RX-left        RX-center        RX-right
```

Same height, same orientation, same distance from the wall, no laptop directly behind/near the center receiver.

7. Move the laptop away from the center receiver  
The laptop near the middle can create center bias through metal, screen, and USB cable geometry. Use longer USB cables if possible and put the laptop behind/aside the receiver row.

Best target layout:

```text
sender side:
          TX
       vertical
   away from railing metal

wall / barrier

receiver side:
RX-left     RX-center     RX-right
same height, same orientation, open air around boards
```

After each geometry change, do a short `chain_check` and compare RSSI. You want the center not to be 10+ dB stronger than left/right. Then rerun the full baseline/left/center/right test.

## User

Ok i will improve the geometry later. For right now lets keep working on the data side. With regards to the repo: What can we do to get better/more detailed data than just left/center/right channel data? Specifically using csi instead of rssi.

## Assistant

Yes. Right now our repo is throwing away most of the CSI detail.

Current pipeline roughly does this in [csi_capture_multi.py](/Users/juliuswenk/Desktop/KD/KZP-inivisible-waves/esp-csi/examples/get-started/tools/csi_capture_multi.py:24):

```text
CSI row -> 192 complex subcarriers -> amplitude list -> average distance from baseline -> one score per receiver
```

So each receiver’s rich CSI packet becomes basically:

```text
left_score
center_score
right_score
```

That is useful for quick zone detection, but not detailed tracking.

Better CSI-based data we can add:

1. **Per-subcarrier activity**
Instead of one score per receiver, keep a vector of changes across all subcarriers:

```text
left[192 values]
center[192 values]
right[192 values]
```

This lets us see which frequencies react to body movement. Some subcarriers are noisy; some are useful.

2. **CSI waterfall view**
Add a live visualization showing CSI amplitude over time:

```text
x-axis: subcarrier index
y-axis: time
brightness: amplitude change from baseline
```

This would show actual signal structure, not just a blob.

3. **Motion energy per receiver over time**
Keep rolling curves:

```text
left_motion
center_motion
right_motion
```

This helps distinguish still presence from movement.

4. **Use phase information**
Currently we mostly use amplitude:

```python
math.hypot(i, q)
```

But CSI has complex values: I/Q. Phase can contain motion information. Raw phase is noisy, but we can try:

```text
phase difference between adjacent subcarriers
phase variance over time
unwrapped phase change
```

This may improve sensitivity, but it is more fragile than amplitude.

5. **PCA / low-dimensional CSI shape**
Instead of 192 values per receiver, reduce CSI into a few components:

```text
receiver left: PC1, PC2, PC3
receiver center: PC1, PC2, PC3
receiver right: PC1, PC2, PC3
```

This gives more detail than left/center/right while staying visualizable.

6. **Train a small classifier**
Collect labeled samples:

```text
empty
left
left-center
center
center-right
right
near
far
walking
still
```

Then train a simple model on CSI features:

```text
logistic regression
random forest
SVM
k-nearest neighbors
```

Not deep learning. Just enough to learn the real room geometry.

Most useful next repo step:

Create a new script:

```text
csi_feature_viewer.py
```

It should display:

```text
top: live blob / zone estimate
middle: three receiver motion curves
bottom: CSI waterfall for selected receiver
side: feature values / confidence
```

And a new capture format that saves extracted features per frame:

```csv
timestamp,receiver,rssi,mean_amp,motion_energy,presence_score,subcarrier_0,...,subcarrier_191
```

That would move us from “which receiver changed most?” to “what does the CSI pattern look like over time?” which is the path toward more detailed tracking.

#!/usr/bin/env python3

import argparse
import ast
import csv
import math
import sys
import time

from PyQt5 import QtCore, QtGui, QtWidgets


DEFAULT_RECEIVERS = {
    "left": "/dev/cu.usbmodem1101",
    "center": "/dev/cu.usbmodem101",
    "right": "/dev/cu.usbmodem2101",
}

ZONE_ORDER = ("left", "center", "right")


def clamp(value, low=0.0, high=1.0):
    return max(low, min(high, value))


def parse_csi_line(line):
    start = line.find("CSI_DATA")
    if start < 0:
        return None
    try:
        row = next(csv.reader([line[start:].strip()]))
        values = ast.literal_eval(row[-1])
        amps = [
            math.hypot(values[i + 1], values[i])
            for i in range(0, len(values) - 1, 2)
        ]
        if not amps:
            return None
        return {
            "rssi": int(row[3]),
            "timestamp": int(row[18]),
            "mean_amp": sum(amps) / len(amps),
            "amps": amps,
        }
    except Exception:
        return None


def average_vector(frames):
    width = min(len(frame) for frame in frames)
    return [
        sum(frame[i] for frame in frames) / len(frames)
        for i in range(width)
    ]


def vector_distance(a, b):
    width = min(len(a), len(b))
    if width == 0:
        return 0.0
    return sum(abs(a[i] - b[i]) for i in range(width)) / width


class MultiCsiWorker(QtCore.QThread):
    values = QtCore.pyqtSignal(dict)
    status = QtCore.pyqtSignal(str)

    def __init__(
        self,
        receivers,
        baud,
        calibration_frames,
        activity_scale,
        emit_interval,
        score_alpha,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.receivers = receivers
        self.baud = baud
        self.calibration_frames = calibration_frames
        self.activity_scale = activity_scale
        self.emit_interval = emit_interval
        self.score_alpha = score_alpha
        self.running = True

    def stop(self):
        self.running = False

    def run(self):
        import serial

        streams = {}
        state = {
            label: {
                "baseline_frames": [],
                "baseline": None,
                "score": 0.0,
                "raw": 0.0,
                "rows": 0,
                "rssi": 0,
            }
            for label in ZONE_ORDER
        }
        last_emit = 0.0

        try:
            for label, port in self.receivers.items():
                streams[label] = serial.Serial(port, self.baud, timeout=0.005)
                self.status.emit(f"{label} opened on {port}")

            while self.running:
                for label, stream in streams.items():
                    raw_line = stream.readline().decode("utf-8", "replace")
                    parsed = parse_csi_line(raw_line)
                    if not parsed:
                        continue

                    item = state[label]
                    item["rows"] += 1
                    item["rssi"] = parsed["rssi"]
                    amps = parsed["amps"]

                    if item["baseline"] is None:
                        item["baseline_frames"].append(amps)
                        if len(item["baseline_frames"]) >= self.calibration_frames:
                            item["baseline"] = average_vector(item["baseline_frames"])
                            item["baseline_frames"] = []
                            self.status.emit(f"{label} calibrated")
                        continue

                    item["raw"] = vector_distance(amps, item["baseline"])
                    item["score"] = (
                        (1.0 - self.score_alpha) * item["score"]
                        + self.score_alpha * item["raw"]
                    )

                now = time.monotonic()
                if now - last_emit < self.emit_interval:
                    continue
                last_emit = now
                self.values.emit(self.make_payload(state))
        except Exception as exc:
            self.status.emit(f"error: {exc}")
        finally:
            for stream in streams.values():
                try:
                    stream.close()
                except Exception:
                    pass

    def make_payload(self, state):
        ready = {
            label: state[label]["baseline"] is not None
            for label in ZONE_ORDER
        }
        progress = {
            label: 1.0
            if ready[label]
            else clamp(len(state[label]["baseline_frames"]) / self.calibration_frames)
            for label in ZONE_ORDER
        }

        scores = {
            label: max(0.0, state[label]["score"])
            for label in ZONE_ORDER
        }
        total = sum(scores.values())
        if total <= 0:
            confidence = {label: 1.0 / len(ZONE_ORDER) for label in ZONE_ORDER}
        else:
            confidence = {
                label: scores[label] / total
                for label in ZONE_ORDER
            }

        winner = max(confidence, key=confidence.get)
        activity = clamp(max(scores.values(), default=0.0) / self.activity_scale)

        return {
            "ready": all(ready.values()),
            "progress": progress,
            "scores": scores,
            "confidence": confidence,
            "winner": winner,
            "activity": activity,
            "rows": {label: state[label]["rows"] for label in ZONE_ORDER},
            "rssi": {label: state[label]["rssi"] for label in ZONE_ORDER},
        }


class ZoneBlobView(QtWidgets.QWidget):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.setWindowTitle("Invisible Waves")
        self.resize(1180, 720)
        self.setMinimumSize(780, 480)
        self.payload = {
            "ready": False,
            "progress": {label: 0.0 for label in ZONE_ORDER},
            "confidence": {label: 1.0 / len(ZONE_ORDER) for label in ZONE_ORDER},
            "winner": "center",
            "activity": 0.0,
        }
        self.blob_x = 0.5

        self.confidence_threshold = 0.48
        self.activity_threshold = 0.22

            }

#!/usr/bin/env python3

import argparse
import ast
import csv
import json
import math
import os
import statistics as stats
import threading
import time
from pathlib import Path


DEFAULT_RECEIVERS = {
    "left": "/dev/cu.usbmodem1101",
    "center": "/dev/cu.usbmodem101",
    "right": "/dev/cu.usbmodem2101",
}


def parse_csi(line):
    start = line.find("CSI_DATA")
    if start < 0:
        return None
    try:
        row = next(csv.reader([line[start:].strip()]))
        values = ast.literal_eval(row[-1])
        amps = [
            math.hypot(values[i + 1], values[i])
            for i in range(0, len(values) - 1, 2)
        ]
        return {
            "rssi": int(row[3]),
            "timestamp": int(row[18]),
            "mean_amp": sum(amps) / len(amps),
            "amps": amps,
        }
    except Exception:
        return None


def capture_port(label, port, baud, duration, output_path, result):
    import serial

    lines = []
    valid = 0
    started = time.time()
    try:
        with serial.Serial(port, baud, timeout=0.2) as stream:
            while time.time() - started < duration:
                line = stream.readline().decode("utf-8", "replace").strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                lines.append(line)
                if parse_csi(line):
                    valid += 1
        output_path.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n")
        result[label] = {"ok": True, "lines": len(lines), "valid": valid}
    except Exception as exc:
        result[label] = {"ok": False, "error": str(exc)}


def load_frames(path):
    frames = []
    for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
        parsed = parse_csi(line)
        if parsed:
            frames.append(parsed)
    return frames


def average_vector(frames):
    if not frames:
        return []
    width = min(len(frame["amps"]) for frame in frames)
    return [
        sum(frame["amps"][i] for frame in frames) / len(frames)
        for i in range(width)
    ]


def vector_distance(a, b):
    width = min(len(a), len(b))
    if width == 0:
        return 0.0
    return sum(abs(a[i] - b[i]) for i in range(width)) / width


def capture(args):
    receivers = dict(DEFAULT_RECEIVERS)
    if args.receiver:
        for item in args.receiver:
            label, port = item.split("=", 1)
            receivers[label] = port

    outdir = Path(args.outdir)
    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    print(f"{args.phase} starts in {args.delay}s", flush=True)
    time.sleep(args.delay)
    print(f"recording {args.phase} for {args.duration}s", flush=True)

    result = {}
    threads = []
    for label, port in receivers.items():
        output_path = outdir / f"{args.phase}_{label}.csv"
        thread = threading.Thread(
            target=capture_port,
            args=(label, port, args.baud, args.duration, output_path, result),
            daemon=True,
        )
        thread.start()
        threads.append(thread)

    for thread in threads:
        thread.join()

    summary_path = outdir / f"{args.phase}_summary.json"
    summary_path.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2) + "\n")
    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2), flush=True)


def analyze(args):
    outdir = Path(args.outdir)
    receivers = list(DEFAULT_RECEIVERS.keys())
    baseline = {}
    for label in receivers:
        frames = load_frames(outdir / f"{args.baseline}_{label}.csv")
        baseline[label] = average_vector(frames)

    rows = []
    for phase in args.phase:
        scores = {}
        for label in receivers:
            frames = load_frames(outdir / f"{phase}_{label}.csv")
            avg = average_vector(frames)
            distances = [
                vector_distance(frame["amps"], baseline[label])
                for frame in frames
            ]
            scores[label] = {
                "rows": len(frames),
                "presence_mean": vector_distance(avg, baseline[label]),
                "presence_p95": sorted(distances)[int(0.95 * len(distances))]
                if distances
                else 0.0,
                "rssi_mean": stats.mean(frame["rssi"] for frame in frames)
                if frames
                else 0.0,
            }
        total = sum(item["presence_mean"] for item in scores.values()) or 1.0
        confidence = {
            label: scores[label]["presence_mean"] / total
            for label in receivers
        }
        winner = max(confidence, key=confidence.get)
        rows.append({"phase": phase, "winner": winner, "confidence": confidence, "scores": scores})

    print(json.dumps(rows, indent=2), flush=True)


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)

    capture_parser = sub.add_parser("capture")
    capture_parser.add_argument("--phase", required=True)
    capture_parser.add_argument("--duration", type=float, default=12)
    capture_parser.add_argument("--delay", type=float, default=5)
    capture_parser.add_argument("--baud", type=int, default=921600)
    capture_parser.add_argument("--outdir", default="captures/multi_zone_test")
    capture_parser.add_argument("--receiver", action="append")
    capture_parser.set_defaults(func=capture)

    analyze_parser = sub.add_parser("analyze")
    analyze_parser.add_argument("--outdir", default="captures/multi_zone_test")
    analyze_parser.add_argument("--baseline", default="baseline")
    analyze_parser.add_argument("--phase", action="append", required=True)
    analyze_parser.set_defaults(func=analyze)

    args = parser.parse_args()
    args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
